entity.name: UK AI Safety Institute
entity.description: Government-funded technical body, originated at Bletchley Summit 2023. Pre-deployment evals on frontier models under voluntary lab agreements; tightest counterpart to NIST AISI. UK's distinctive non-statutory "context-specific" AI approach gives AISI outsized policy weight.

activity.title: OpenAI and Microsoft join UK's international coalition to safeguard AI development
activity.raw_text: Leading tech firms OpenAI and Microsoft are the latest to join an initiative spearheaded by the UK's AI Security Institute (AISI) - encouraging trust and public confidence in AI as it rewires public services and drives national renewal. Announced by Deputy Prime Minister David Lammy, and AI Minister Kanishka Narayan as the AI Impact Summit in India draws to a close today (Friday 20 February), the news bolsters the work of AISI's Alignment Project which was first announced last summer.

_verify_phrases: spearheaded by the UK's AI Security Institute

enrichment.summary: The UK AI Security Institute (AISI) announced that OpenAI and Microsoft have joined its Alignment Project, an international coalition initiative aimed at building trust and public confidence in AI development. The announcement was made by Deputy Prime Minister David Lammy and AI Minister Kanishka Narayan at the close of the AI Impact Summit in India on 20 February 2026. The Alignment Project was first announced the previous summer and seeks to safeguard AI development through international cooperation.
enrichment.stance_quote: encouraging trust and public confidence in AI as it rewires public services and drives national renewal

activity.title: AI Security Institute – Frontier AI Trends report factsheet
activity.raw_text: The UK AI Security Institute (AISI) has conducted evaluations of frontier AI systems since November 2023 across domains critical to national security and public safety. The AI Security Institute – Frontier AI Trends report presents our first public analysis of the trends we've observed. It seeks to provide accessible, data-driven insights into the frontier of AI capabilities and promote a shared understanding among governments, industry, and the public.

_verify_phrases: first public analysis of the trends we've observed

enrichment.summary: The UK AI Security Institute (AISI) published its Frontier AI Trends report factsheet, presenting its first public analysis of trends observed from evaluations of frontier AI systems conducted since November 2023. The report covers domains critical to national security and public safety. It aims to provide data-driven insights into frontier AI capabilities for governments, industry, and the public.
